scenes.title: Regular Polygon
scenes.description: Tests the `regular` substructure on `animated_polygon` and `polygon`. Use the Doc tab for full reference.
scenes.markdown:
# Regular Polygon

Both `polygon` (static) and `animated_polygon` support a `regular` substructure that computes vertices for a regular N-gon instead of requiring an explicit vertex list.

---

## `polygon` — Static

Use when the shape is **fixed at scene-load time** and never needs to change.

Geometry is built once. **Zero per-frame cost.**

```json
{
  "type": "polygon",
  "regular": { "n": 6, "radius": 1, "center": [0, 0, 0], "rotation": 0 },
  "color": "#66cc88",
  "opacity": 0.4,
  "outlineWidth": 2
}
```

All fields inside `regular` are **plain numbers**.

---

## `animated_polygon` — Live Expressions

Use when the shape must **respond to sliders, time $t$, or scene functions**.

Geometry is rebuilt every frame. Expressions evaluated on each update.

```json
{
  "type": "animated_polygon",
  "regular": { "n": "N", "radius": "R", "center": ["cx","cy","0"], "rotation": "t" },
  "opacity": "fill_opacity",
  "outlineWidth": "outline_width",
  "outlineOpacity": "outline_opacity"
}
```

All fields inside `regular` are **expression strings**. `opacity`, `outlineWidth`, and `outlineOpacity` also accept expressions.

---

## `regular` Fields

| Field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `n` | `3` | Number of sides — clamped to $\max(3, \text{round}(n))$ |
| `radius` | `1` | Circumradius — distance from center to each vertex |
| `center` | `[0,0,0]` | Center position |
| `rotation` | `0` | Rotation offset in radians |

---

## Outline Fields

| Field | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `outlineWidth` | `1.5` (regular) / `0` (explicit) | Line width in pixels. Uses MathBox geometry-based rendering — works in WebGL |
| `outlineColor` | inherits `color` | Outline color |
| `outlineOpacity` | `min(1, fillOpacity × 2)` | Independent opacity. When omitted, tracks fill opacity |

---

## When to Use Which

| Situation | Use |
|---|---|
| Fixed reference shape, background geometry | `polygon` |
| Shape driven by a slider | `animated_polygon` |
| N-gon converging to a circle | `animated_polygon` |
| Rotation driven by time $t$ | `animated_polygon` |
| Many polygons, performance matters | `polygon` where possible |

**Rule of thumb:** start with `polygon`. Switch to `animated_polygon` only when interactivity or animation is required.

---

## Performance

- **`polygon`** — one-time geometry build at load. No updater. Static GPU mesh.
- **`animated_polygon`** — updater fires ~60×/sec: expression eval → rebuild geometry → GPU upload → compute normals → outline update.

steps.title: N-gon converges to a circle as N → ∞
steps.description: Drag **N** to watch a regular N-gon reshape in real time. At large N the polygon approaches a circle and its perimeter approaches 2π. Each field inside `regular` is a live expression re-evaluated every frame.

steps.title: Live: all four regular fields (n, R, center, rotation)
steps.description: All four `regular` fields driven independently: **N** (sides), **R** (radius), **center** (x, y). Rotation is wired to wall-clock time `t` — it spins continuously without a slider. Drag any control to see its effect in isolation.

info.content: $$N={{N}},\quad R={{R}},\quad \text{center}=({{cx}},\,{{cy}}) \qquad P={{toFixed(N * 2 * R * sin(pi / N), 3)}} \qquad A={{toFixed(0.5 * N * R * R * sin(2 * pi / N), 3)}}$$

steps.title: Static polygon — geometry built once at load
steps.description: Three static polygons (triangle, hexagon, octagon) built once at load — no updater, no per-frame cost. Use `polygon` with `regular` for fixed background geometry where interactivity isn't needed.

steps.title: 3D — Hexagonal helix
steps.description: Six static hexagons arranged in a helix: each step rotates 60° around the Z axis and rises by 0.3 in z. The color shifts warm → cool as you ascend. A small central hexagon marks the axis.

info.content: Helix: $\theta_k = k\cdot 60°$,\; $z_k = k/4 - 0.75$,\; $r_{\text{helix}} = 1.2$ — six hexagons, one full turn.
